questions.explanation: 'x' is the letter representing a value that can change, so it is the "variable" (biến số).

questions.question: In the equation $x + 5 = 10$, what is the "constant"?
questions.explanation: '5' is a fixed value that does not change, so it is a "constant" (hằng số).

questions.question: "Difference between two numbers" means you should ...
questions.explanation: "Difference" (hiệu) means the result of subtraction (phép trừ).

questions.question: What is the integer $5 - 8$? (Start at 5, count back 8)
questions.explanation: Starting at 5 and moving left 8 steps on the number line brings you to -3.

questions.question: What is the integer $-2 + 6$? (Start at -2, count on 6)
questions.explanation: Starting at -2 and moving right 6 steps on the number line brings you to 4.

questions.question: A number that is less than zero is [[negative|positive|integer]].
questions.explanation: "Negative" means "số âm", which is less than zero.

questions.question: The number 7 is [[positive|negative|variable]].
questions.explanation: 7 is greater than zero, so it is "positive" (số dương).

questions.question: The letter 'y' in $y + 3 = 5$ is a [[variable|constant|integer]].
questions.explanation: 'y' is a letter representing a value that can change, so it is a "variable" (biến số).

questions.question: Which two numbers are integers?
questions.explanation: -12 and 9 are integers (số nguyên). 0.8 is a decimal. 3/4 is a fraction.

questions.question: A ... is a material used to make things.
questions.explanation: "Substance" (chất) is the general term for a material.

questions.question: What are the three "states" of water?
questions.explanation: The three states of matter are solid, liquid, and gas. For water, these are ice, water, and steam.

questions.question: The temperature at which a solid becomes a liquid is the ...
questions.explanation: "Melting point" (độ nóng chảy) is the temperature for changing from solid to liquid.

questions.question: The temperature at which a liquid has enough energy to change to a gas is the ...
questions.explanation: "Boiling point" (nhiệt độ sôi) is the temperature for changing from liquid to gas.

questions.question: When all the particles in a liquid have enough energy to become a gas, the liquid will ...
questions.explanation: "Boil" (sôi) is when the change to gas happens throughout the entire liquid, not just the surface.

questions.question: When only the particles on the surface of a liquid change into a gas, the liquid will ...
questions.explanation: "Evaporate" (bay hơi) is when the change to gas happens only on the surface, usually below the boiling point.

questions.question: A ... is something about a substance that is different from other substances (e.g., color, hardness).
questions.explanation: "Property" (đặc tính) is a characteristic of a substance.

questions.question: How much matter an object contains is its ...
questions.explanation: "Mass" (khối lượng) is the measure of how much matter is in an object.

questions.question: All objects ... (chiếm không gian).
questions.explanation: "Take up space" có nghĩa là "chiếm không gian".

questions.question: "State" in science means ...
questions.explanation: "State" (trạng thái) refers to solid, liquid, or gas.

questions.question: "Melting point" in Vietnamese is ...
questions.explanation: "Melting point" có nghĩa là "độ nóng chảy".

questions.question: "Boiling point" in Vietnamese is ...
questions.explanation: "Boiling point" có nghĩa là "nhiệt độ sôi".

questions.question: "Property" in Vietnamese is ...
questions.explanation: "Property" có nghĩa là "đặc tính".

questions.question: "Mass" in Vietnamese is ...
questions.explanation: "Mass" có nghĩa là "khối lượng".

questions.question: Ice is the ... state of water.
questions.explanation: Ice is the "solid" (rắn) state of water.

questions.question: Steam is the ... state of water.
questions.explanation: Steam (hơi nước) is the "gas" (khí) state of water.

questions.question: Match the term to its definition.
rightCol: How much matter an object contains
The material used to make things

answer.Mass: How much matter an object contains
answer.Substance: The material used to make things

questions.explanation: These are key vocabulary terms from Science.

questions.question: Match the process to its description.
rightCol: Liquid turns to gas throughout the whole liquid
Liquid turns to gas only on the surface
The temperature when a solid turns to liquid
The temperature when a liquid turns to gas

answer.Boil: Liquid turns to gas throughout the whole liquid
answer.Evaporate: Liquid turns to gas only on the surface
answer.Melting point: The temperature when a solid turns to liquid
answer.Boiling point: The temperature when a liquid turns to gas

questions.explanation: These are terms for changes of state.

questions.question: The change from solid to liquid is called [[melting|boiling]]. The change from liquid to gas is called [[boiling|melting]].
questions.explanation: Solid to liquid is melting (nóng chảy). Liquid to gas is boiling (sôi).

questions.question: Water turning into ice is called freezing. Ice turning into water is called ...
questions.explanation: The opposite of freezing (đông đặc) is "melting" (nóng chảy).

questions.question: Water turning into steam is called boiling. Steam turning into water is called ...
questions.explanation: The opposite of boiling (sôi) is "condensing" (ngưng tụ).

questions.question: The three states of matter are solid, liquid, and ...
questions.explanation: "Gas" (khí) is the third state of matter.

questions.question: Hardness, color, and boiling point are all examples of a ...
questions.explanation: These are all "properties" (đặc tính) of a substance.

questions.question: All matter has ... and takes up space.
questions.explanation: The two defining characteristics of all matter are that it has "mass" (khối lượng) and "takes up space" (chiếm không gian).

questions.question: Hardness, color, and state are all examples of a substance's ...
questions.explanation: These are all "properties" (đặc tính) of a substance.

questions.question: Water at $50^{\circ}C$ is in which state?
questions.explanation: The melting point of water is $0^{\circ}C$ and the boiling point is $100^{\circ}C$. $50^{\circ}C$ is between these two points, so it is a "liquid" (lỏng).

questions.question: Water at $110^{\circ}C$ is in which state?
questions.explanation: $110^{\circ}C$ is above the boiling point of water ($100^{\circ}C$), so it is in the "gas" (khí) state (steam).

questions.question: Water at $-5^{\circ}C$ is in which state?
questions.explanation: $-5^{\circ}C$ is below the melting/freezing point of water ($0^{\circ}C$), so it is in the "solid" (rắn) state (ice).

questions.question: The ... of water is $100^{\circ}C$.
questions.explanation: $100^{\circ}C$ is the "boiling point" (nhiệt độ sôi) of water.

questions.question: The ... of ice is $0^{\circ}C$.
questions.explanation: $0^{\circ}C$ is the "melting point" (độ nóng chảy) of ice.

questions.question: A wet towel becomes dry after being left in the sun. This is an example of ...
questions.explanation: The water on the towel's surface turns into gas (water vapor) and goes into the air. This is "evaporation" (bay hơi).

questions.question: "Gases expand to fill their container." This is a property of gases.
questions.explanation: Đúng. This is a key property of gases mentioned in the reading section: they expand (giãn nở) to fill any container they are in.

questions.question: What is the difference between "boil" and "evaporate"?

options.a: No difference, they are the same.
options.b: Boiling happens at a specific temperature ($100^{\circ}C$ for water), evaporation can happen at any temperature.
options.c: Boiling happens on the surface, evaporation happens throughout the liquid.
options.d: Boiling is liquid to gas, evaporation is solid to gas.

questions.explanation: Boiling is a specific event at the boiling point. Evaporation (bay hơi) happens on the surface at any temperature (e.g., a puddle drying up on a cool day).

questions.question: All matter has two main properties: It has ... and it ....
questions.explanation: The two defining properties of all matter are having "mass" (khối lượng) and "taking up space" (chiếm không gian).

questions.question: "Something about a substance that is different from other substances." This is the definition of ...
questions.explanation: "Property" (đặc tính) is a characteristic.

questions.question: "A positive or negative number or 0, but not a decimal or fraction." This is the definition of ...
questions.explanation: "Integer" (số nguyên).

questions.question: "The organ inside your chest that pumps blood." This is the ...
questions.explanation: "Heart" (Tim).

questions.question: "The sport of hitting a shuttlecock over a net." This is ...
questions.explanation: "Badminton" (Cầu Lông).

questions.question: "A warm cover for your bed." This is a ...
questions.explanation: "Blanket" (cái chăn).

questions.question: "A house in the countryside." This is a ...
questions.explanation: "Country house" (nhà ở nông thôn).

questions.question: "This is my teacher's desk." This is correct grammar.
questions.explanation: "teacher" (singular) uses 's.

questions.question: "The dogs's house is small." This is correct grammar.
questions.explanation: "dogs" (plural ending in s) should use s'. Correct is "The dogs' house".

questions.question: What is -3 minus 4? (Start at -3, count back 4)
questions.explanation: Start at -3 and move 4 steps to the left: -4, -5, -6, -7.

questions.question: What is -5 add 5? (Start at -5, count on 5)
questions.explanation: Start at -5 and move 5 steps to the right. This brings you to 0.

questions.question: Which two numbers are NOT integers?
questions.explanation: 2.5 is a decimal and 1/2 is a fraction. Integers cannot be decimals or fractions.

questions.question: Read the passage: "Basketball and volleyball are two popular sports. Both are played with a ball and a net. However, in basketball, you bounce the ball, but in volleyball, you hit it." This passage mainly uses which skills?
questions.explanation: The passage uses "Both" (Comparing) to show similarities and "However" / "but" (Contrasting) to show differences.

questions.question: "To tell how things are alike" is to ...
questions.explanation: "Compare" (so sánh) means to find similarities.

questions.question: "To tell how things are different" is to ...
questions.explanation: "Contrast" (tương phản) means to find differences.

questions.question: "To put things in order" is ...
questions.explanation: "Sequencing" (Sắp xếp thứ tự) is about order.

questions.question: Arrange the steps for making tea, based on sequencing logic.
muc: Next, put the tea bag in a cup.
Then, pour the hot water into the cup.
answer: Next, put the tea bag in a cup.
Then, pour the hot water into the cup.
questions.explanation: This is a logical sequence of events.

questions.question: Nối từ vựng với nghĩa tiếng Việt tương ứng.
questions.explanation: Đây là các từ vựng cơ bản về bộ phận cơ thể.

questions.question: Nối từ vựng với nghĩa tiếng Việt tương ứng.
questions.explanation: Đây là các từ vựng cơ bản về bộ phận cơ thể.

questions.question: You wear a watch on your ...
questions.explanation: Đồng hồ thường được đeo ở cổ tay, là một phần của "arm".

questions.question: You use your ... to see.
questions.explanation: "Eye" (mắt) là cơ quan thị giác, dùng để nhìn.

questions.question: You use your ... to smell.
questions.explanation: "Nose" (mũi) là cơ quan khứu giác, dùng để ngửi.

questions.question: You use your ... to listen to music.
questions.explanation: "Ear" (tai) là cơ quan thính giác, dùng để nghe.

questions.question: A giraffe has a very long ...
questions.explanation: Hươu cao cổ nổi tiếng với "neck" (cái cổ) rất dài.

questions.question: Humans have ten ... on their hands.
questions.explanation: "Fingers" (ngón tay) là bộ phận trên bàn tay.

questions.question: He kicked the ball with his left ...
questions.explanation: Chúng ta dùng "leg" (chân) để đá bóng.

questions.question: She wears a beautiful necklace around her ...
questions.explanation: Vòng cổ (necklace) được đeo quanh "neck" (cổ).

questions.question: What is the opposite of "tall"?
questions.explanation: "Tall" (cao) là từ trái nghĩa của "short" (lùn, thấp).

questions.question: A person who is slightly fat in a pleasant way is called ...
questions.explanation: "Chubby" (mập mạp, mũm mĩm) dùng để mô tả người hơi mập một cách dễ thương.

questions.question: Someone who is good at sports and looks fit is ...
questions.explanation: "Sporty" (dáng thể thao) mô tả người trông khỏe mạnh và yêu thích thể thao.

questions.question: "Appearance" là một từ tiếng Anh có nghĩa là ...
questions.explanation: "Appearance" (Ngoại hình) là từ vựng chủ đề của bài.

questions.question: My brother is very [[tall|chubby|short]], he is 1.8 meters.
questions.explanation: 1.8 mét là một chiều cao ấn tượng, vì vậy "tall" (cao) là từ phù hợp.

questions.question: The baby has ... cheeks.
questions.explanation: Trẻ em thường có má "chubby" (mũm mĩm).

questions.question: She is very ...; she plays football and volleyball.
questions.explanation: Người chơi nhiều môn thể thao thường có "sporty" (dáng thể thao).

questions.question: He is not tall, he is ...
questions.explanation: Từ trái nghĩa của "tall" (cao) là "short" (thấp).

questions.question: The general look of a person is called their ...
questions.explanation: "Appearance" (Ngoại hình) là dáng vẻ bên ngoài của một người.

questions.question: "He is tall" is a sentence describing ...
questions.explanation: "Tall" (cao) là một từ dùng để mô tả "Appearance" (Ngoại hình).

questions.question: "Chubby" is a word used to describe someone's ...
questions.explanation: "Chubby" (mũm mĩm) là một từ mô tả ngoại hình.

questions.question: "Sporty" means looking ...
questions.explanation: "Sporty" (dáng thể thao) có nghĩa là trông khỏe mạnh ("fit") và năng động ("active").

questions.question: A ... person is shorter than average.
questions.explanation: "Short" (thấp) là từ dùng để mô tả người có chiều cao dưới trung bình.

questions.question: Which two words describe appearance?
questions.explanation: "Tall" (cao) và "Chubby" (mũm mĩm) dùng để mô tả ngoại hình. "Arm" và "Eye" là các bộ phận cơ thể.

questions.question: "My friend has a sporty appearance." This sentence is correct.
questions.explanation: Câu này đúng ngữ pháp, "sporty" là tính từ bổ nghĩa cho danh từ "appearance".

questions.question: Nối từ vựng (Personality) với nghĩa tiếng Việt tương ứng.
questions.explanation: Đây là các tính từ chỉ tính cách tích cực.

questions.question: Nối từ vựng (Personality) với nghĩa tiếng Việt tương ứng.
questions.explanation: Đây là các tính từ chỉ tính cách tích cực.

questions.question: Nối từ vựng (Personality) với nghĩa tiếng Việt tương ứng.
questions.explanation: Đây là các tính từ chỉ tính cách.

questions.question: A person who is ... always works a lot and doesn't give up.
questions.explanation: "Hard-working" (chăm chỉ) mô tả người làm việc siêng năng.

questions.question: My friend is very ...; I can trust her with my secrets.
questions.explanation: "Reliable" (đáng tin cậy) là người mà bạn có thể tin tưởng.

questions.question: He is very ... and always has new ideas for his projects.
questions.explanation: "Creative" (sáng tạo) là người có nhiều ý tưởng mới.

questions.question: She is a ... person; she always looks before she crosses the road.
questions.explanation: "Careful" (cẩn thận) là người luôn chú ý để tránh nguy hiểm hoặc sai sót.

questions.question: A person who likes to give money or gifts to others is ...
questions.explanation: "Generous" (rộng rãi, hào phóng) là người thích cho đi.

questions.question: What is the opposite of "serious"?
questions.explanation: "Serious" (nghiêm túc) là từ trái nghĩa của "funny" (thú vị, hài hước).

questions.question: What is the opposite of "shy"?
questions.explanation: "Shy" (bẽn lẽn, hay xấu hổ) là từ trái nghĩa của "confident" (tự tin).

questions.question: "He is very active" means he ...

options.c: likes to do sports and move around.

questions.explanation: "Active" (năng động) mô tả người thích vận động.

questions.question: My cat is very ...; it always wants to know what is in the box.
questions.explanation: "Curious" (tò mò) là muốn tìm hiểu, khám phá.

questions.question: She is [[sensitive|caring|clever]]. She often cries when watching sad movies.
questions.explanation: "Sensitive" (nhạy cảm) mô tả người dễ bị ảnh hưởng bởi cảm xúc.

questions.question: He is a [[reliable|freedom-loving|creative]] friend. You can trust him.
questions.explanation: "Reliable" (đáng tin cậy) là một người bạn mà bạn có thể tin tưởng.

questions.question: He is a very ... student; he gets top marks in all his exams.
questions.explanation: "Clever" (thông minh) mô tả người học nhanh và đạt điểm cao.

questions.question: A teacher needs to be ... with young children.
questions.explanation: "Patient" (kiên nhẫn) là một đức tính quan trọng khi làm việc với trẻ nhỏ.

questions.question: She has a ... personality. (FRIEND)
questions.explanation: Cần một tính từ (adjective). "Friendly" (thân thiện) là tính từ của "friend" (bạn).

questions.question: He doesn't like rules. He is very ... (FREEDOM-LOVING)
questions.explanation: "Freedom-loving" (yêu tự do) là tính từ ghép mô tả người không thích các quy tắc.

questions.question: She is a very ... person. (CARE)
questions.explanation: Cần một tính từ (adjective). "Caring" (quan tâm) là tính từ mô tả người.

questions.question: Phân loại các tính từ chỉ tính cách sau.
questions.explanation: Friendly, hard-working, active là các tính cách tích cực. Shy và serious có thể là trung lập.

questions.question: Phân loại các tính từ chỉ tính cách sau.
questions.explanation: Clever, creative, curious liên quan đến suy nghĩ. Caring, generous liên quan đến cảm xúc và cách đối xử với người khác.

questions.question: Chọn 2 từ trái nghĩa.
questions.explanation: "Hard-working" (chăm chỉ) và "Lazy" (lười biếng) là hai từ trái nghĩa.

questions.question: Chọn 2 từ đồng nghĩa hoặc gần nghĩa.
questions.explanation: "Clever" (lanh lợi) và "Smart" (thông minh) là hai từ gần nghĩa.

questions.question: "He is a serious person." This sentence means he is not ...
questions.explanation: "Serious" (nghiêm túc) thường là trái nghĩa với "funny" (hài hước).

questions.question: A person who is ... does not like to be with other people.
questions.explanation: "Shy" (bẽn lẽn, hay xấu hổ) là người cảm thấy không thoải mái khi ở cùng người khác.

questions.question: A person who helps people or an organization without being paid.
questions.explanation: "Volunteer" (tình nguyện viên) là người làm việc không nhận lương.

questions.question: An event in which people compete to win.
questions.explanation: "Competition" (cuộc thi) là một sự kiện có cạnh tranh.

questions.question: A group of people who sing together.
questions.explanation: "Choir" (dàn đồng ca) là một nhóm hát.

questions.question: A fast competition, often with cars, bikes, or people running.
questions.explanation: "Racing" (cuộc đua) là một cuộc thi về tốc độ.

questions.question: A building where interesting and valuable old things are collected and shown to the public.
questions.explanation: "Museum" (viện bảo tàng) là nơi trưng bày các hiện vật.

questions.question: To get ready for an event.
questions.explanation: "Prepare" (chuẩn bị) là sẵn sàng cho một việc gì đó.

questions.question: To plan or arrange an event.
questions.explanation: "Organise" (tổ chức) là lên kế hoạch và sắp xếp.

questions.question: A meal cooked outdoors over an open fire or grill.
questions.explanation: "Barbecue" (món thịt nướng) là một bữa ăn được nấu ngoài trời.

questions.question: This film is not interesting at all. It is ...
questions.explanation: "Boring" (buồn tẻ) là tính từ mô tả sự không thú vị.

questions.question: The 12 signs of astrology (like Leo, Taurus, Cancer) are called the ...
questions.explanation: "Zodiac" (cung hoàng đạo) là 12 cung chiêm tinh.

questions.question: We are going to have a ... in the park on Sunday.
questions.explanation: "Barbecue" (tiệc nướng) thường được tổ chức ngoài trời, ví dụ như ở công viên.

questions.question: My school is going to ... a sports competition.
questions.explanation: "Organise" (tổ chức) là động từ đi với "competition" (cuộc thi).

questions.question: He is a ... at the local hospital, helping sick children.
questions.explanation: "Volunteer" (tình nguyện viên) là người giúp đỡ người khác mà không cần trả công.

questions.question: She is ... (PREPARE) for her exam.
questions.explanation: Dùng thì Hiện tại tiếp diễn (is preparing) để mô tả hành động đang diễn ra.

questions.question: I want to join the school ... because I love singing.
questions.explanation: "Choir" (dàn đồng ca) là nơi dành cho những người thích hát.

questions.question: Nối từ vựng với nghĩa tiếng Việt tương ứng.
questions.explanation: Đây là các từ vựng về hoạt động và sự kiện.

questions.question: Nối từ vựng với nghĩa tiếng Việt tương ứng.
questions.explanation: Đây là các danh từ khác trong bài.

questions.question: Phân loại các danh từ sau.
questions.explanation: "Volunteer" (tình nguyện viên) là một người. "Competition" (cuộc thi) là một sự kiện. "Museum" (viện bảo tàng) là một nơi chốn. "Choir" (dàn đồng ca) là một nhóm người.

questions.question: Phân loại các động từ và danh từ.
questions.explanation: "Organise" và "prepare" là động từ. "Barbecue", "racing", "competition" là danh từ (chỉ sự kiện).

questions.question: "My club is ... (ORGANISE) a barbecue." This sentence is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: Sai. Cần có động từ 'to be' đi kèm V-ing. Câu đúng: "My club **is** organising a barbecue."

questions.question: [[I am|I is|I are]] hard-working.
questions.explanation: The verb 'to be' for 'I' is 'am'.

questions.question: [[She is|She am|She are]] generous.
questions.explanation: The verb 'to be' for 'She' is 'is'.

questions.question: [[They are|They is|They am]] friendly.
questions.explanation: The verb 'to be' for 'They' is 'are'.

questions.question: My friends ... (BE) very clever.
questions.explanation: "My friends" is plural (like "they"), so we use "are".

questions.question: He ... (NOT / BE) shy. He is confident.
questions.explanation: The negative form of 'is' is 'is not' (or 'isn't').

questions.question: ... your brother tall?
questions.explanation: "Your brother" is singular (like "he"), so we use "Is" to start the question.

questions.question: I ... long black hair.
questions.explanation: To talk about possession (sở hữu), we use 'have' for 'I'.

questions.question: She ... a round face.
questions.explanation: To talk about possession (sở hữu), we use 'has' for 'She', 'He', 'It'.

questions.question: They ... (NOT / HAVE) sporty appearances.
questions.explanation: The negative of 'have' for 'They' is 'do not have' (or 'don't have').

questions.question: ... she ... a round face?
questions.explanation: To make a question with 'have' for 'she', we use the auxiliary verb "Does" and the base form "have".

questions.question: Look! She [[is coming|comes|come]].
questions.explanation: "Look!" indicates an action happening right now, which uses the Present Continuous.

questions.question: I [[am reading|read|reads]] a book now.
questions.explanation: "now" indicates an action happening right now, which uses the Present Continuous.

questions.question: My sister ... (PLAY) the piano at the moment.
questions.explanation: "at the moment" indicates an action happening right now, which uses the Present Continuous.

questions.question: They ... (NOT / DO) their homework. They are watching TV.
questions.explanation: The second sentence ("They are watching TV") shows the action is happening now. The negative form is "are not doing".

questions.question: What ... you ... (READ)?
questions.explanation: This is a question about an action happening now, using the Present Continuous.

questions.question: "He have blue eyes." This sentence is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: Sai. For 'He', we must use "has". Correct: "He has blue eyes."

questions.question: "She is not hard-working." This sentence is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: Đúng. Đây là dạng phủ định đúng của động từ 'to be' (Subject + is not + adjective).

questions.question: "I am talk on the phone." This sentence is grammatically correct.
questions.explanation: Sai. The Present Continuous tense requires V-ing. Correct: "I am talking on the phone."

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a question.
questions.explanation: Cấu trúc câu hỏi Yes/No với 'have': Does + S + have + O?

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
questions.explanation: Cấu trúc Present Continuous: S + are + V-ing + ...

questions.question: ... do they study? (Họ học ở đâu?)
questions.explanation: "Where" được dùng để hỏi về nơi chốn (ở đâu).

questions.question: ... do you watch on TV? (Bạn xem gì trên TV?)
questions.explanation: "What" được dùng để hỏi về sự vật, sự việc (cái gì).

questions.question: ... do these plants eat other living things? (Tại sao...)
questions.explanation: "Why" được dùng để hỏi về lý do (tại sao).

questions.question: The Antarctic ... the fifth biggest continent. (Là lục địa...)
questions.explanation: Đây là sự thật hiển nhiên (Present Simple). "The Antarctic" (Nó) đi với "is".

questions.question: It ... the largest single mass of ice of Earth.
questions.explanation: Đây là sự thật hiển nhiên (Present Simple). "It" (Nó) đi với "has".

questions.question: Not many species ... in the Antarctic.
questions.explanation: Đây là sự thật hiển nhiên (Present Simple). "Not many species" (Nhiều loài - số nhiều) đi với động từ "live" nguyên mẫu.

questions.question: The ice [[is melting|melts|melt]].
questions.explanation: Cần dùng thì Hiện tại tiếp diễn (is melting) để nói về "changing situations which are happening now" (những tình huống đang thay đổi diễn ra ngay lúc nói).

questions.question: Penguins [[are suffering|suffer|suffers]] from the effects of climate change.
questions.explanation: Cần dùng thì Hiện tại tiếp diễn (are suffering) để nói về "changing situations which are happening now". "Penguins" (số nhiều) đi với "are".

questions.question: ... do you watch on TV? (Bạn xem gì...)
questions.explanation: "What" (cái gì) dùng để hỏi về sự vật (chương trình TV).

questions.question: ... do they study? (Họ học ở đâu?)
questions.explanation: "Where" (ở đâu) dùng để hỏi về nơi chốn.

questions.question: ... do these plants eat other living things? (Tại sao...)
questions.explanation: "Why" (tại sao) dùng để hỏi về lý do.

questions.question: The Antarctic ... (BE) the fifth biggest continent.
questions.explanation: Dùng "is" cho thì Hiện tại đơn, chủ ngữ số ít (The Antarctic).

questions.question: It ... (HAVE) the largest single mass of ice of Earth.
questions.explanation: Dùng "has" cho thì Hiện tại đơn, chủ ngữ số ít (It).

questions.question: Not many species ... (LIVE) in the Antarctic.
questions.explanation: Dùng "live" (nguyên mẫu) cho thì Hiện tại đơn, chủ ngữ số nhiều (species).

questions.question: The ice ... (MELT) right now.
questions.explanation: Dùng "is melting" (Hiện tại tiếp diễn) vì có "right now".

questions.question: Match the word to its Vietnamese meaning.
questions.explanation: Đây là các từ vựng liên quan đến chủ đề "Plants" (Thực vật).

questions.question: Match the word to its Vietnamese meaning.
questions.explanation: Đây là các danh từ trong chủ đề "Plants".

questions.question: Nối các thuật ngữ trong chuỗi thức ăn.
rightCol: Sinh vật sản xuất (vd: cây cỏ)
Sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 1 (ăn cây cỏ)
Sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 2 (ăn sinh vật bậc 1)
Sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 3 (ăn sinh vật bậc 2)

answer.Producer: Sinh vật sản xuất (vd: cây cỏ)
answer.Primary consumer: Sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 1 (ăn cây cỏ)
answer.Secondary consumer: Sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 2 (ăn sinh vật bậc 1)
answer.Tertiary consumer: Sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 3 (ăn sinh vật bậc 2)

questions.explanation: Đây là cấu trúc của một chuỗi thức ăn (food chain).

questions.question: Nối các từ vựng về động vật.
questions.explanation: Đây là các từ vựng dùng để mô tả đặc điểm động vật.

questions.question: A carnivorous plant has to ... insects.
questions.explanation: Cây ăn thịt phải "trap" (bẫy) côn trùng.

questions.question: The sticky liquid on the plant's leaves helps to ... insects.
questions.explanation: Chất lỏng "sticky" (dính) giúp "trap" (bẫy) côn trùng.

questions.question: The plant's ... (dịch vị) will ... (tiêu hóa) the insect.
questions.explanation: "Juices" (dịch vị) của cây sẽ "digest" (tiêu hóa) con mồi.

questions.question: Sugar ... in water. (Đường ... trong nước)
questions.explanation: "Dissolve" (tan, rã) là từ đúng khi nói đường tan trong nước.

questions.question: To get the juice, you must ... the fruit.
questions.explanation: Bạn phải "crush" (nghiền nát) trái cây để lấy nước ép.

questions.question: In a food chain, a rabbit (which eats grass) is a ...
questions.explanation: Con thỏ (rabbit) ăn cỏ (producer), vì vậy nó là "Primary consumer" (sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 1).

questions.question: In a food chain, a fox (which eats rabbits) is a ...
questions.explanation: Con cáo (fox) ăn thỏ (primary consumer), vì vậy nó là "Secondary consumer" (sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 2).

questions.question: A lion is a ... because it hunts zebras.
questions.explanation: Sư tử là "predator" (động vật săn mồi) vì nó săn ngựa vằn.

questions.question: The natural home of an animal is its ...
questions.explanation: "Habitat" (môi trường sống) là ngôi nhà tự nhiên của động vật.

questions.question: A ... is eaten by a primary consumer.
questions.explanation: "Producer" (sinh vật sản xuất) là thức ăn của "primary consumer".

questions.question: A secondary consumer eats a ... consumer.
questions.explanation: Sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 2 (Secondary) ăn sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 1 (primary).

questions.question: A tertiary consumer eats a ... consumer.
questions.explanation: Sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 3 (Tertiary) ăn sinh vật tiêu thụ bậc 2 (secondary).

questions.question: Fish use ... to breathe underwater.
questions.explanation: Cá dùng "gills" (mang) để thở.

questions.question: Mammals and birds use ... to breathe air.
questions.explanation: Thú và chim dùng "lungs" (phổi) để thở.

questions.question: A bird's body is covered in ...
questions.explanation: Cơ thể chim được bao phủ bởi "feathers" (lông vũ).

questions.question: A mammal's body is covered in ...
questions.explanation: Cơ thể thú (động vật có vú) được bao phủ bởi "fur" (lông mao).

questions.question: A fish's body is covered in ...
questions.explanation: Cơ thể cá được bao phủ bởi "scales" (vảy).

questions.question: Phân loại các nhóm động vật.
questions.explanation: Rắn và cá sấu là bò sát. Ếch và kỳ nhông là lưỡng cư. Cá mập là cá.

questions.question: Phân loại các nhóm động vật.
questions.explanation: Chim cánh cụt và đại bàng là chim. Dơi và cá voi là thú. Kiến là côn trùng.

questions.question: Sắp xếp một chuỗi thức ăn dưới nước.
muc: Leopard Seal (Hải cẩu báo - Tertiary Consumer)
answer: Leopard Seal (Hải cẩu báo - Tertiary Consumer)
questions.explanation: Tảo bị tép ăn, tép bị chim cánh cụt ăn, và chim cánh cụt bị hải cẩu báo ăn.

questions.question: "A plant uses gills to breathe." (Cây dùng mang để thở.)
questions.explanation: Sai. Plants "breathe" (trao đổi khí) through pores (khí khổng) in their leaves, not gills. Fish have gills.

questions.question: Nối từ vựng Toán học với nghĩa tiếng Việt.
rightCol: Chia đôi (chia hai nửa bằng nhau)

answer.Bisect: Chia đôi (chia hai nửa bằng nhau)

questions.explanation: Đây là các thuật ngữ chính trong Unit 6 Maths.

questions.question: Nối từ vựng Hình học với nghĩa tiếng Việt.
questions.explanation: Đây là các thuật ngữ Hình học (Geometry) trong danh sách.

questions.question: Trong phân số 5/8, số 5 được gọi là ...
questions.explanation: "Numerator" (Tử số) là số ở trên của phân số.

questions.question: Trong phân số 3/4, số 4 được gọi là ...
questions.explanation: "Denominator" (Mẫu số) là số ở dưới của phân số.

questions.question: A line that joins opposite corners of a quadrilateral is a ...
questions.explanation: "Diagonal" (Đường chéo) là đường nối hai đỉnh đối diện.

questions.question: An instrument used to draw circles is ...
questions.explanation: "Compasses" (Com-pa) là dụng cụ dùng để vẽ hình tròn.

questions.question: The perimeter of a circle is its ...
questions.explanation: "Circumference" (Chu vi) là đường viền bao quanh hình tròn.

questions.question: A line from the centre to the circumference of a circle is the ...
questions.explanation: "Radius" (Bán kính) là đoạn thẳng từ tâm đến đường tròn.

questions.question: A line that cuts a circle exactly in half is the ...
questions.explanation: "Diameter" (Đường kính) là đoạn thẳng đi qua tâm và cắt đôi hình tròn.

questions.question: Two lines that never meet, like railway tracks, are ...
questions.explanation: "Parallel" (Song song) là các đường thẳng không bao giờ gặp nhau.

questions.question: To ... a number means to break it into its parts. For example, $352 = 300 + 50 + 2$.
questions.explanation: "Decompose" (Tách) là hành động phân tách một con số ra thành các phần (trăm, chục, đơn vị).

questions.question: To ... an angle means to cut it exactly in half.
questions.explanation: "Bisect" (Chia đôi) có nghĩa là chia chính xác thành hai phần bằng nhau.

questions.question: "The top number of a fraction is the denominator." (Số trên của phân số là mẫu số.)
questions.explanation: Sai. The top number is the "numerator" (tử số). The bottom number is the "denominator" (mẫu số).

questions.question: "The radius is twice the diameter." (Bán kính dài gấp đôi đường kính.)
questions.explanation: Sai. The diameter is twice the radius (Đường kính dài gấp đôi bán kính).

questions.question: "Parallel lines meet at one point." (Đường thẳng song song gặp nhau tại một điểm.)
questions.explanation: Sai. Parallel (song song) lines never meet.

questions.question: "A diagonal joins two adjacent (next to) corners." (Đường chéo nối hai góc liền kề.)
questions.explanation: Sai. A diagonal joins two *opposite* (đối diện) corners.

questions.question: The top number of a fraction is the ...
questions.explanation: "Numerator" (tử số).

questions.question: The bottom number of a fraction is the ...
questions.explanation: "Denominator" (mẫu số).

questions.question: A ... is a quadrilateral with one pair of parallel sides. (Hình thang)
questions.explanation: "Trapezium" (Hình thang) là hình tứ giác có một cặp cạnh song song.

questions.question: To ... means to give a reason for your answer.
questions.explanation: "Justify" (giải thích, chứng minh) là đưa ra lý do.

questions.question: A geologist is a person [[who|which|whose]] studies rocks.
questions.explanation: We use 'who' for people. 'A geologist' is a person.

questions.question: That is the house [[where|which|when]] I was born.
questions.explanation: We use 'where' for places. 'The house' is a place.

questions.question: I can't find the keys [[which|who|whose]] I left on the table.
questions.explanation: We use 'which' (or 'that') for things. 'The keys' are things.

questions.question: The man [[whose|who|whom]] car was stolen went to the police.
questions.explanation: We use 'whose' to show possession (the car *of* the man).

questions.question: This is the hospital ... my mother works.
questions.explanation: 'Where' is used to refer to a place (the hospital).

questions.question: Do you know the boy ... is talking to Mrs. Lien?
questions.explanation: 'Who' is used to refer to a person (the boy) as the subject of the verb ('is talking').

questions.question: The computer ... I bought last week is already broken.
questions.explanation: 'That' (or 'which') is used to refer to a thing (the computer).

questions.question: I'll never forget the day ... I graduated.
questions.explanation: 'When' is used to refer to a time (the day).

questions.question: She complained to the man [[whose|who|which]] dog bit her.
questions.explanation: 'Whose' shows possession (the dog belonging to the man).

questions.question: A cemetery is a place ... people are buried.
questions.explanation: 'Where' is used to refer to a place (a cemetery).

questions.question: What is the name of the woman ... lives next door?
questions.explanation: 'Who' refers to a person (the woman).

questions.question: A 'gladiator' was a man ... used to fight in the Colosseum.
questions.explanation: 'Who' refers to a person (a man).

questions.question: I don't like stories ... have unhappy endings.
questions.explanation: 'That' (or 'which') refers to things (stories).

questions.question: The 'constitution' is a document ... outlines the basic laws of a society.
questions.explanation: 'Which' (or 'that') refers to a thing (a document).

questions.question: Listen! The birds [[are singing|sing|sings]].
questions.explanation: We use the Present Continuous for actions happening *now* ('Listen!').

questions.question: The Earth [[goes|is going|go]] around the Sun.
questions.explanation: We use the Present Simple for things that are always true (facts).

questions.question: "What are you doing?" - "I [[am reading|read|reads]] my homework."
questions.explanation: The question "What are you doing?" (Present Continuous) asks about an action happening *now*.

questions.question: Penguins [[live|are living|is living]] in the Antarctic.
questions.explanation: We use the Present Simple for general facts or permanent situations.

questions.question: The ice in the Antarctic [[is melting|melts|melt]] because of climate change.
questions.explanation: We use the Present Continuous to talk about changing situations which are happening now.

questions.question: Be quiet! My parents ... (SLEEP).
questions.explanation: "Be quiet!" indicates an action happening right now, so we use the Present Continuous.

questions.question: Water ... (BOIL) at 100 degrees Celsius.
questions.explanation: This is a scientific fact, which always uses the Present Simple.

questions.question: How often ... you ... (VISIT) your grandparents?
questions.explanation: "How often" asks about a repeated habit, which requires the Present Simple.

questions.question: Look at the sky! The clouds ... (GET) darker.
questions.explanation: "Look!" indicates a changing situation happening now.

questions.question: My school classes always ... (START) at 7 AM.
questions.explanation: "always" indicates a routine or schedule, which uses the Present Simple.

questions.question: I ... (NOT / WATCH) TV at the moment. I am doing homework.
questions.explanation: "at the moment" clearly indicates the Present Continuous tense.

questions.question: Match the word to its definition (Nối từ với định nghĩa).
rightCol: The number of times a sound is produced in one second. (tần số)
Equipment to record sound. (Thiết bị ghi âm)
When an egg breaks and a young bird comes out. (nở)
A group of birds that live together. (bầy đàn)

answer.frequency: The number of times a sound is produced in one second. (tần số)
answer.microphone: Equipment to record sound. (Thiết bị ghi âm)
answer.hatch: When an egg breaks and a young bird comes out. (nở)
answer.colony: A group of birds that live together. (bầy đàn)

questions.explanation: These are vocabulary words from the Story and Listening sections.

questions.question: Match the word to its definition (Nối từ với định nghĩa).
rightCol: To break down food in the body. (tiêu hóa)
To mix with a liquid and disappear. (tan, rã)
To catch something so it cannot escape. (bẫy)
Like glue; will attach to things that touch it. (dính, nhớt)
A substance that flows, like water. (chất lỏng)

answer.digest: To break down food in the body. (tiêu hóa)
answer.dissolve: To mix with a liquid and disappear. (tan, rã)
answer.trap: To catch something so it cannot escape. (bẫy)
answer.sticky: Like glue; will attach to things that touch it. (dính, nhớt)
answer.liquid: A substance that flows, like water. (chất lỏng)

questions.explanation: These are vocabulary words from the "Plants" section.

questions.question: A small device used to record information about an activity or location is called a ...
questions.explanation: A tracker (Thiết bị theo dõi) is used to record information about activity.

questions.question: ... is a unit of measurement for sound frequency.
questions.explanation: Hertz (Hz) is the unit of measurement for sound frequency (Là đơn vị đo tần số).

questions.question: Penguins ... together to keep warm in the cold Antarctic weather.
questions.explanation: 'Huddle' (tụ tập lại) means to stand close together to keep warm.

questions.question: A ... is an animal's partner to produce young.
questions.explanation: A 'mate' (bạn đời) is an animal's partner.

questions.question: The ... in your stomach helps to digest food.
questions.explanation: 'Stomach juices' (dịch vị) contain acids that digest food.

questions.question: A pitcher plant has ... leaves to trap insects.
questions.explanation: The leaves are 'sticky' (dính, nhớt) so insects cannot escape.

questions.question: What is the name of the famous Roman arena where gladiators fought?
questions.explanation: The 'Colosseum' (đấu trường La Mã) is the famous arena.

questions.question: A ... was a man who fought to the death in the Colosseum.
questions.explanation: A 'Gladiator' (Đấu sĩ) fought in the arena.

questions.question: Rosa Parks was a ... who refused to give up her seat on a bus.
questions.explanation: A 'Seamstress' (Thợ may vá) is a person who sews. Rosa Parks worked as a seamstress.

questions.question: The unfair treatment of people based on their race is called ...
questions.explanation: 'Discrimination' (sự phân biệt) is the unfair treatment of different categories of people.

questions.question: ... is the system of keeping people separate based on their race.
questions.explanation: 'Segregation' (Sự tách biệt) was the policy of separating black and white people in the US.

questions.question: What do you call the list of items needed to cook a dish?
questions.explanation: 'Ingredients' (Thành phần, nguyên liệu) are the food items needed.

questions.question: The step-by-step instructions on how to cook a dish is called the ...
questions.explanation: A 'Recipe' (Công thức) includes both the ingredients and the directions (instructions).

questions.question: ... is a type of fungus used to make bread rise.
questions.explanation: 'Yeast' (men) is the microorganism that produces gas, making bread fluffy.

questions.question: A ... is a large, luxurious house in the countryside, especially in ancient Rome.
questions.explanation: A 'villa' (Biệt thự) was a large country house.

questions.question: The ... branch of government is responsible for making laws.
questions.explanation: 'Legislative' (lập pháp) means law-making.

questions.question: The ... branch of government is responsible for interpreting laws (like judges and courts).
questions.explanation: 'Judicial' (tư pháp) relates to courts and justice.

questions.question: This person was famous for her ... (COURAGE) in the face of discrimination.
questions.explanation: We need a noun ('courage' - sự can đảm) in this position.

questions.question: A ... (SEAMSTRESS) is a woman who sews for a living.
questions.explanation: This is a definition. 'Seamstress' (Thợ may vá).

questions.question: An ... (ORATOR) is a skilled public speaker.
questions.explanation: An 'Orator' (Diễn giả, nhà hùng biện) is a public speaker.

questions.question: The set of basic laws and principles for a country is called the ...
questions.explanation: The 'Constitution' (hiến pháp) is the highest law of a country.

questions.question: Match the U.S. government branch to its function.
questions.explanation: These are the three branches of the US government mentioned in the vocabulary list.

questions.question: Categorize these words from the "Rosa Parks" story.
questions.explanation: 'Courage' and 'Justice' are positive. 'Discrimination' and 'Segregation' are negative.

questions.question: A place where you buy vegetables and fruit. (Nơi bạn mua rau củ quả)
questions.explanation: A 'Grocery' is a 'cửa hàng rau củ quả'.

questions.question: A place where trains stop for passengers. (Nơi tàu hỏa dừng cho hành khách)
questions.explanation: A 'Railway station' is a 'nhà ga'.

questions.question: A large, open area in a town, often surrounded by buildings. (Một khu vực mở, rộng lớn trong thị trấn)
questions.explanation: A 'Square' is a 'quảng trường'.

questions.question: A shop that sells medicine. (Cửa hàng bán thuốc)
questions.explanation: A 'Pharmacy' is a 'hiệu thuốc'.

questions.question: A large building where paintings and other works of art are shown to the public.
questions.explanation: An 'Art gallery' (phòng trưng bày nghệ thuật) is where art is displayed.

questions.question: A place where people are buried when they die.
questions.explanation: A 'Cemetery' (nghĩa trang) is a place for burial.

questions.question: The home of a king or queen.
questions.explanation: A 'Palace' (cung điện) is the official residence of a monarch.

questions.question: An area on the edge of a large town or city, where people live.
questions.explanation: A 'Suburb' (khu vực ngoại ô) is a residential area outside the city center.

questions.question: A road or path that is not wide is called ...
questions.explanation: 'Narrow' (hẹp) is the opposite of 'wide' (rộng).

questions.question: To go to the post office, go ... ahead and then turn left.
questions.explanation: 'Go straight ahead' (đi thẳng về phía trước) is a common direction.

questions.question: My house is on the ... side of the street, opposite the park.
questions.explanation: 'Right' (phải) and 'left' (trái) are used to indicate sides.

questions.question: A place that sells fuel (gas) for cars.
questions.explanation: A 'Petrol station' (trạm xăng dầu) sells fuel for cars.

questions.question: A place where firefighters work and keep their equipment.
questions.explanation: A 'Fire station' (trạm cứu hỏa) is the base for firefighters.

questions.question: A place where you can see a doctor if you are sick, but it's not a hospital.
questions.explanation: A 'Health centre' (trung tâm y tế) provides medical services.

questions.question: A large store selling many types of goods (clothes, electronics, food...).
questions.explanation: A 'Department store' (cửa hàng tạp hóa / bách hóa) sells many different 'departments' of items.

questions.question: A shop that sells clothes, especially for women.
questions.explanation: A 'Dress shop' (cửa hàng váy) sells dresses and other clothing.

questions.question: A shop that sells second-hand goods given by people to raise money for a good cause.
questions.explanation: A 'Charity shop' (cửa hàng từ thiện) sells donated items for charity.

questions.question: A person who cuts men's hair.
questions.explanation: A 'Barber' (thợ cắt tóc nam) traditionally cuts men's hair.

questions.question: A place where you can get your hair cut or styled.
questions.explanation: A 'Hairdresser's' (hiệu cắt tóc) is a salon for hair.

questions.question: A place where people go to have treatments to improve their appearance (e.g., facials, manicures).
questions.explanation: A 'Beauty salon' (tiệm làm đẹp) focuses on cosmetic treatments.

questions.question: A building where people make or repair things.
questions.explanation: A 'Workshop' (phân xưởng) is a room or building for making or repairing goods.

questions.question: A structure built to remind people of a famous person or event.
questions.explanation: A 'Memorial' (đài tưởng niệm) is built to honor a memory.

questions.question: A very large and important church.
questions.explanation: A 'Cathedral' (nhà thờ lớn, thánh đường) is the principal church of a diocese.

questions.question: A building for Buddhist worship.
questions.explanation: A 'Pagoda' (ngôi chùa) is a building for Buddhist worship.

questions.question: A building used for the worship of a god or gods (general term).
questions.explanation: A 'Temple' (đền, điện, miếu) is a general term for a building of worship.

questions.question: A building for Christian worship.
questions.explanation: A 'Church' (nhà thờ) is a building for Christian worship.

questions.question: The garden or area at the back of a house.
questions.explanation: A 'Backyard' (sân phía sau nhà) is the area behind a house.

questions.question: A school for students aged approximately 11 to 15.
questions.explanation: 'Secondary school' (trường trung học cơ sở) comes after primary school.

questions.question: A large building for public Christian worship, often the main church of a diocese.
questions.explanation: A 'Cathedral' (nhà thờ lớn, thánh đường) is a large, important church.

questions.question: The area behind a house is the ...
questions.explanation: 'Backyard' (sân phía sau nhà).

questions.question: An open area in a town, like Ba Dinh ...
questions.explanation: Ba Dinh Square (Quảng trường Ba Đình).

questions.question: A place where buses stop for people to get on or off.
questions.explanation: 'Bus stop' (trạm xe buýt).

questions.question: A man who cuts men's hair is a ...
questions.explanation: 'Barber' (thợ cắt tóc nam).

questions.question: A place that sells goods given by people to raise money for a good cause.
questions.explanation: 'Charity shop' (cửa hàng từ thiện).

questions.question: A place where people go for medical help; not a hospital.
questions.explanation: 'Health centre' (trung tâm y tế).

questions.question: Match the adjective to its opposite (Nối tính từ với từ trái nghĩa).
questions.explanation: These are pairs of antonyms (từ trái nghĩa) used to describe places.

questions.question: My neighborhood is very ... because it is near a main road. There is always traffic.
questions.explanation: A main road with traffic causes a lot of noise, so the neighborhood is 'noisy' (ồn ào).

questions.question: The beach is very ..., with fine yellow sand.
questions.explanation: A beach is characterized by 'sand' (cát), so it is 'sandy' (phủ đầy cát).

questions.question: Hoi An is a ... town with many old houses.
questions.explanation: 'Historic' (cổ, có tính lịch sử) is used to describe places that are old and important in history.

questions.question: The air in the city is very ... because of the smoke from factories.
questions.explanation: Smoke from factories causes 'pollution', making the air 'polluted' (ô nhiễm).

questions.question: The countryside is very ... and quiet. I like to go there to relax.
questions.explanation: 'Peaceful' (yên bình) is the best word to describe a quiet and relaxing place.

questions.question: The market is always ... on weekends, with too many people.
questions.explanation: 'Crowded' (đông đúc) means full of people.

questions.question: The new supermarket is very ... It's near my house, so I can walk there.
questions.explanation: 'Convenient' (thuận tiện) means easy to use or reach.

questions.question: The bus stop is far from my house. It's very ...
questions.explanation: 'Inconvenient' (bất tiện) is the opposite of 'convenient'.

questions.question: I had a ... holiday. Everything was perfect!
questions.explanation: 'Fantastic' (tuyệt vời) is a strong positive adjective.

questions.question: The weather was .... We had to stay indoors all day.
questions.explanation: 'Terrible' (tồi tệ) is a strong negative adjective.

questions.question: The theme park was ...! I want to go again.
questions.explanation: 'Exciting' (thú vị, lý thú) describes something that makes you feel happy and enthusiastic.

questions.question: The Statue of Liberty in New York is a very famous ...
questions.explanation: A 'Statue' (tượng) is an image of a person or animal made of stone or metal.

questions.question: The new building is ... tall. I can't believe it. (INCREDIBLE)
questions.explanation: We need an adverb ('incredibly' - đáng kinh ngạc) to modify the adjective ('tall').

questions.question: She ... (DISLIKE) washing the dishes. She hates it.
questions.explanation: 'Dislike' (không thích, ghét) is a verb. For 'She' (Present Simple), we add -s.

questions.question: My house is ... (SMALL) than your house.
questions.explanation: This is a comparative adjective. 'small' is a short adjective, so we add '-er'.

questions.question: This book is ... (INTERESTING) than that book.
questions.explanation: This is a comparative adjective. 'interesting' is a long adjective (3 syllables), so we use 'more'.

questions.question: A motorbike is ... (FAST) than a bicycle.
questions.explanation: 'fast' is a short adjective, so we add '-er'.

questions.question: My new neighborhood is ... (PEACEFUL) than my old one.
questions.explanation: 'peaceful' is a 2-syllable adjective ending in 'ful', so we treat it as a long adjective and use 'more'.

questions.question: The city is ... (POLLUTED) than the countryside.
questions.explanation: 'polluted' is a 3-syllable adjective, so we use 'more'.

questions.question: The new shopping mall is ... (MODERN) than the old market.
questions.explanation: 'modern' is a 2-syllable adjective, we use 'more'.

questions.question: The library is ... (QUIET) than the town square.
questions.explanation: 'quiet' is a short adjective, so we add '-er'.

questions.question: This street is ... (NARROW) than that avenue.
questions.explanation: 'narrow' is a 2-syllable adjective ending in 'ow', so we can add '-er'.

questions.question: I think living in the countryside is ... (GOOD) than living in the city.
questions.explanation: 'good' is an irregular comparative adjective. The comparative form is 'better'.

questions.question: The weather today is ... (BAD) than it was yesterday.
questions.explanation: 'bad' is an irregular comparative adjective. The comparative form is 'worse'.

questions.question: This test was ... (DIFFICULT) than the last one.
questions.explanation: 'difficult' is a 3-syllable (long) adjective, so we use 'more'.

questions.question: My brother is [[taller|more tall|tall]] than me.
questions.explanation: 'tall' is a short adjective, so we add '-er'.

questions.question: A car is [[more expensive|expensiver|expensive]] than a bicycle.
questions.explanation: 'expensive' is a long adjective, so we use 'more'.

questions.question: The food at this restaurant is [[worse|badder|more bad]] than the food at the cafe.
questions.explanation: 'bad' is an irregular adjective; its comparative form is 'worse'.

questions.question: Health is [[more important|importanter|important]] than money.
questions.explanation: 'important' is a long adjective, so we use 'more'.

questions.question: Match the word to its Vietnamese meaning (Nối từ với nghĩa).
questions.explanation: These are key terms from the Science vocabulary list.

questions.question: A scientist who studies rocks.
questions.explanation: A 'Geologist' (nhà địa chất) is a scientist who studies rocks.

questions.question: A rock that is made up of crystals is called ...
questions.explanation: A 'Crystalline' rock is a rock made of crystals.

questions.question: The process where liquid magma or lava cools down and becomes solid rock.
questions.explanation: 'Solidification' (sự đông đặc) is the process of becoming solid.

questions.question: A rock which comes from magma that has cooled down.
questions.explanation: 'Igneous rock' (đá lửa) is formed from cooled magma or lava.

questions.question: ... igneous rock has formed from lava cooling down ON the surface of the Earth.
questions.explanation: 'Extrusive' (phun trào) means it is pushed *out* of the Earth. 'Ex-' means 'out'.

questions.question: ... igneous rock has formed when magma cools down INSIDE the Earth's crust.
questions.explanation: 'Intrusive' (xâm nhập) means it stays *inside*. 'In-' means 'inside'.

questions.question: When we see something with our eyes without the help of a magnifying glass.
questions.explanation: 'Naked eye' (mắt thường) means seeing without optical aids.

questions.question: A natural substance in a rock with a crystalline structure.
questions.explanation: A 'Mineral' (khoáng chất) is a component of a rock.

questions.question: The particular shaped structure of a mineral.
questions.explanation: A 'Crystal' (pha lê, kết tinh) is the specific structure of a mineral.

questions.question: A geologist is a scientist who studies ...
questions.explanation: A 'Geologist' (nhà địa chất) studies rocks.

questions.question: ... is the process of a liquid turning into a solid.
questions.explanation: 'Solidification' (Sự đông đặc).

questions.question: If you can see it without a telescope, you can see it with the ...
questions.explanation: 'Naked eye' (mắt thường).

questions.question: ... rock is formed from cooled magma inside the Earth.
questions.explanation: 'Intrusive' (xâm nhập) means inside.

questions.question: ... rock is formed from lava on the Earth's surface.
questions.explanation: 'Extrusive' (phun trào) means outside.

Lines that do not meet and stay the same distance apart. (song song)

answer.Diagonal: A line that joins opposite corners. (đường chéo)
answer.Parallel: Lines that do not meet and stay the same distance apart. (song song)

answer.Radius: A line joining the centre to the circumference. (bán kính)
answer.Circumference: The perimeter of a circle. (chu vi hình tròn)

questions.explanation: A 'Mixed number' (hỗn số) is a whole number and a proper fraction combined.

questions.question: A line that joins the opposite vertices (corners) of a square is called a ...
questions.explanation: A 'Diagonal' (đường chéo) joins opposite corners.

questions.question: Two lines on a train track are ... to each other.
questions.explanation: 'Parallel' (song song) lines never meet.
'Compasses' (com-pa) is the tool used for drawing circles.

questions.question: The distance around the outside of a circle is its ...
questions.explanation: The 'Circumference' (chu vi) is the perimeter of a circle.

questions.question: The plural of 'radius' is ...
questions.explanation: This is an irregular plural from Latin. 'Radii' is the plural of 'radius'.

questions.question: The line from the center of a circle to its edge is the ...
questions.explanation: 'Radius' (bán kính).

questions.question: The line that cuts a circle in half, passing through the center, is the ...
questions.explanation: 'Diameter' (đường kính).

questions.question: The middle point of a circle is the ...
questions.explanation: 'Centre' (tâm).

questions.question: To break down a number like 456 into $400 + 50 + 6$ is to ... it.
questions.explanation: 'Decompose' (tách) means to break down into parts.

questions.question: A large, open area in a city, often with buildings around it, is called a...
questions.explanation: A "Square" (quảng trường) is a large, open area in a city[cite: 95].

questions.question: You go to a ... to buy medicine.

questions.explanation: A "Railway station" (nhà ga) is for trains[cite: 95].

questions.question: You go to a [[Petrol station|Pharmacy|Charity shop]] to buy fuel for a car.

questions.question: An area on the edge of a city, away from the center, is the ...

questions.question: What is the opposite of "Left" (trái)?

questions.question: This street is too [[narrow|straight|left]] for a bus. It's also very [[noisy|sandy|historic]] because of the traffic.
questions.explanation: A street can be too "narrow" (hẹp) for a bus [cite: 97] and "noisy" (ồn ào) from traffic[cite: 100].

questions.question: Categorize these adjectives.
questions.explanation: Peaceful and Exciting are positive[cite: 100]. Crowded, Polluted, and Terrible are negative[cite: 100].

questions.question: A "Historic" place is...
questions.explanation: "Historic" (cổ, có tính lịch sử) means something is old and important[cite: 100].

questions.question: "Đèn giao thông" in English is ...
questions.explanation: "Traffic light" is the translation for "đèn giao thông"[cite: 100].

questions.question: A ... is a statue built to remember people or events.
questions.explanation: A "Memorial" (đài tưởng niệm) is built to remember someone or something[cite: 97].

questions.explanation: "Square" is the translation for "quảng trường"[cite: 95].

questions.explanation: Narrow and Historic describe the place itself[cite: 97, 100]. Exciting, Fantastic, and Terrible describe a person's feeling about it[cite: 100, 103].

questions.explanation: "Charity shop" is the translation for "cửa hàng từ thiện"[cite: 95].

questions.explanation: A "Barber" (thợ cắt tóc) is a place for men's haircuts[cite: 95].

questions.question: A "tiệm làm đẹp" in English is a ...
questions.explanation: "Beauty salon" is the translation for "tiệm làm đẹp"[cite: 97].

questions.question: The adverb "incredibly" (đáng kinh ngạc) is used to describe a ...
questions.explanation: "Incredibly" (đáng kinh ngạc) describes the *degree* or *level* of something (e.g., "incredibly noisy")[cite: 100].

questions.explanation: A "Church" (nhà thờ) is a Christian place of worship[cite: 97].

questions.explanation: "Temple" is the translation for "đền, điện, miếu"[cite: 97].

questions.question: To "dislike" something means you find it "exciting".
questions.explanation: Sai. "Dislike" (không thích) is a negative feeling, while "Exciting" (thú vị) is positive[cite: 100, 103].

questions.question: "Terrible" (tồi tệ) and "Fantastic" (tuyệt vời) are antonyms (trái nghĩa).
questions.explanation: Đúng. "Terrible" is very bad, and "Fantastic" is very good[cite: 100, 103].

questions.question: A "suburb" (ngoại ô) is the center of the city.
questions.explanation: Sai. A suburb is an area on the *edge* of a city[cite: 97].

questions.question: My new apartment is [[more convenient|convenienter|less convenient]] than my old one. It is closer to the bus stop.
questions.explanation: "Convenient" is a long adjective, so its comparative form is "more convenient"[cite: 100, 103].

questions.question: This old street is [[narrower than|more narrow than|narrow than]] the main road.
questions.explanation: "Narrow" is a short adjective, so its comparative form is "narrower than"[cite: 97, 103].

questions.question: Which word is a short adjective (using '-er' for comparative)?
questions.explanation: "Tall" becomes "taller"[cite: 103]. The others are long adjectives ("more modern", "more historic", "more crowded")[cite: 100, 103].

questions.question: Which word is a long adjective (using 'more' for comparative)?
questions.explanation: "Polluted" is a long adjective ("more polluted")[cite: 100, 103]. "Tall" is short ("taller")[cite: 103]. "Left" and "Straight" are directions[cite: 97].

questions.question: This homework is (bad) ... than the last one.
questions.explanation: "Bad" is an irregular adjective. Its comparative form is "worse" (tồi tệ hơn).

questions.question: "Trạm cứu hỏa" in English is ...
questions.explanation: "Fire station" is the translation for "trạm cứu hỏa"[cite: 95].

questions.question: A "Cửa hàng tạp hóa" (selling many types of goods) is a ...
questions.explanation: "Department store" is the translation for "cửa hàng tạp hóa"[cite: 95].

questions.question: Which relative pronoun refers to people (as a subject)?
questions.explanation: "Who" refers to people as a subject (e.g., "The man who lives there...")[cite: 100].

questions.question: Which relative pronoun refers to animals and objects?
questions.explanation: "Which" refers to animals and objects (e.g., "The dog which barked...")[cite: 100].

questions.question: Which relative pronoun refers to possession?
